steps.title: Substitute y = 16 into both equations
steps.content:
Since the intersection point is (q, 16), we know $y = 16$ satisfies both equations.

First equation: $-x - w(16) = -361$ becomes $x + 16w = 361$
Second equation: $2x - w(16) = 50$ becomes $2x - 16w = 50$

steps.title: Add equations to eliminate w
steps.content:
Adding both equations cancels the $w$ terms:
$$(x + 16w) + (2x - 16w) = 361 + 50$$
$$3x = 411$$
$$x = 137$$
